name: library_book_ban_algorithm_minor_access
premise: County librarian director Alex Marsh must implement a new board mandate to auto-flag books containing sexual content for minor cardholders. Teen services librarian Nora Alvarez shows the keyword model bans a Pulitzer-winning novel about war assault survivors while missing illustrated grooming guides shelved in nonfiction. Strict enforcement satisfies conservative board members; loosening rules risks funding cuts and public accusations of exposing children. The model vendor refuses liability for false positives.

agents.name: Alex Marsh
agents.identity: I am the county library director accountable to an elected board, professional ethics codes, and diverse community access goals.
agents.goal: I want minors protected from predatory material without letting crude filters erase LGBTQ health texts and survivor literature.

agents.name: Nora Alvarez
agents.identity: I am a teen services librarian who runs confidential health information sessions and sees which books actually help adolescents disclose abuse.
agents.goal: I want manual curation with teen input instead of automated bans that mirror political panic more than real risk.

choices: I will deploy the vendor model as mandated and publish appeal pathways, accepting documented false positives until the next board meeting.
I will override auto-bans with a human appeals desk that can restore flagged titles same-day, accepting personal firing risk if the board interprets that as insubordination.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The board chair demands screenshots of enforcement by morning. Alex Marsh must choose between strict automated bans or a same-day human override desk. What does Alex Marsh do?
